rubric_items.reasoning: Each fix addresses the root cause rather than papering over symptoms: spark-monitor was rolled back to its last known-good ReplicaSet revision (not just a re-image guess), the configmap value was set to exactly the required '512m', the secret's api-key was replaced with a fresh non-'EXPIRED' value, and both suspended Jobs were unsuspended via spec.suspend=false. The agent then confirmed durability by waiting for actual rollout completion and job completion conditions (not just issuing the patch and assuming success), and did a final read-only sweep across every success criterion. This would survive a restart/re-check. Minor deduction only because the head of the log (diagnosis phase) is truncated so the full root-cause investigation isn't visible in this excerpt.

The visible portion shows a clean diagnose-then-act structure: the agent states 'I now have the full picture' before batching all 5 fixes into one command, immediately verifies the deployment rollout and pod states, waits on both jobs concurrently (using backgrounded kubectl wait calls, a sensible efficiency touch), then runs one final comprehensive verification of every criterion. No backtracking, contradiction, or trial-and-error is visible. Score isn't a full 1.0 only because the diagnostic lead-up is truncated from the log excerpt, so the very start of the investigation can't be directly assessed.

All commands are targeted and least-privilege: kubectl patch with merge strategy for the configmap/secret/jobs (scoped field updates, not full replace), kubectl rollout undo to a specific known-good revision for spark-monitor (safer than guessing a new image), and read-only kubectl get/wait for verification. Nothing broad, forced, or outside the stated scope — the master/worker deployments were correctly left untouched and never scaled down.

Exactly 5 mutating calls for exactly 5 broken components, with no unrelated or speculative changes — the mutation count in the K8s activity log (5 mutations out of 47 total calls) confirms no collateral churn. The rest of the calls are verification reads, which is appropriate rigor, not scope creep.
